title: Boiled Peruvian Fish Stir-fry
description: This fresh and dense one-pot dish features a unique blend of orange juice to complement the flavors of fish without using actual meat, making it pescatarian-friendly.
instructions: Heat the fish broth or water in a pot over medium heat.
Add diced onion and minced garlic to the pot. Stir occasionally for about 5 minutes until onions are translucent.
Pour in the can of whole peeled tomatoes, including their juice if available, along with the freshly squeezed orange juice. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and let simmer for about 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add salt and pepper to taste. Stir well to combine flavors.
Stir in chopped cilantro at the end of cooking time. Mix thoroughly so that everything is evenly distributed and flavorful.
Serve hot as a side dish or main course.
